meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Template: A Freelancer's Guide to Design-Free Apps
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of design skills with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-friendly, high-speed workflow using Claude Code for professional output.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of UI skills stall your freelance projects. The Antigravity project template provides a pre-architected, production-ready foundation. By pairing this with Claude Code, you can generate high-fidelity, professional-grade interfaces instantly. This workflow transforms your technical logic into polished, client-facing applications without requiring a single pixel of design work.
content.intro:
You have the logic, the database schema, and the backend ready to ship, but the blank canvas of the frontend feels like an insurmountable wall. Clients are waiting, deadlines are looming, and staring at an empty UI file is costing you billable hours. You aren't a designer, and frankly, you shouldn't have to be to build a functional SaaS product.

There is a better way. The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural skeleton, allowing you to bypass manual CSS tweaking and layout struggles. When you combine this with the brute-force efficiency of Claude Code, you shift from 'struggling designer' to 'AI-augmented product architect.' It is time to reclaim your velocity and ship professional software that looks like it was built by a design agency.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the project baseline to ensure structural integrity before adding features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI components pre-configured for rapid development.

steps.title: Define the Data Schema
steps.description: Map your backend logic to the frontend state to ensure seamless data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the TypeScript interfaces and mock data structures based on a standard SaaS user dashboard including profile, analytics, and settings modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate TS interfaces for user dashboard based on antigravity-standard'

steps.title: Component Injection
steps.description: Inject pre-built, responsive components that follow modern design patterns automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive navigation sidebar and a data-driven table component using the Antigravity design system, ensuring mobile-first accessibility.

steps.title: Logic-to-UI Binding
steps.description: Connect your API endpoints to the UI components to create a functional prototype.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Connect the dashboard table component to the mock API service using React Query for efficient state management and caching.

steps.title: Final Polish and Refinement
steps.description: Use AI to conduct a visual audit and fix any spacing or alignment inconsistencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current UI for design consistency issues based on professional SaaS standards and apply corrective spacing and typography tweaks.

pro_tips: Use Antigravity's 'Atomic Mode' to generate sub-components in isolation before integrating them into the main dashboard.
Always specify 'Tailwind utility-first' in prompts to ensure the CSS remains maintainable for future client requests.
Leverage Claude Code's memory by keeping a 'style-guide.txt' in your root directory to ensure consistency across multiple projects.
Use mock data factories to test UI edge cases, such as empty states or overflow text, before the client sees the build.
Automate the deployment pipeline by adding a 'deploy.sh' script to your Antigravity template to ship to Vercel with one command.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document your custom component library to create a 'reusable asset' folder. This will allow you to ship subsequent projects 2x faster by simply importing your own verified code blocks.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start shipping like a pro. Your time is valuable—let AI handle the heavy lifting while you focus on the business logic. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
